data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion G6 (Approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection) [agent]. Target: the "Eval foundation: deterministic-scorer registry + per-reviewer dataset schema (2.1 + 2.3)" plan (relates to this ticket's shipped eval suite). Tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/{eval.py,contracts.py} and the shipped eval_specs/. ONE coaching finding (shape-validation source-of-truth); mechanism, alternative-selection, and positive rationale otherwise PASS.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):

- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — solves the NAMED problem, not a proxy. The "What" names a real gap: validate_scorer's deterministic branch today returns [] for ANY non-empty name (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:120-123), so a typo'd `recall_on_seeded_defcts` passes the offline gate silently. A name->pure-function registry + tightened validate_scorer closes exactly that hole (AC#2). Mechanism maps 1:1 to the goal.

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (data-flow boundary handled honestly). The registry's `(case, structured_output) -> bool/score` signature is well-formed for offline scoring, and the plan correctly scopes itself to offline + deterministic (AC#5: "No live model calls"). I verified the live scoring path is deliberately deferred — run_eval raises NotImplementedError and assembles the Inspect Task "from spec" in the eval CI, not offline (eval.py:319-351). So building the registry as offline substrate the (not-yet-wired) harness will later consume is a coherent boundary, not an unreachable-code defect — the offline-testable discipline (validate_eval_spec/validate_scorer) is exactly what gates locally per the module's stated posture (eval.py:19-21). PASS, not a G6 mechanism finding.

- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; generated alternatives discarded). I generated two structurally-different alternatives: (a) inline/ad-hoc per-spec scoring with no central table, and (b) a class/plugin-based scorer hierarchy. The plan's name->pure-function TABLE is at-least-as-good on every axis: codebase-alignment (mirrors the existing `{type,name}` scorer-dict shape AND the shipped `contracts.register_contract`/`response_model_for` registry pattern, contracts.py:24-39), blast-radius (stdlib-only pure functions, no new class surface), testability (each function unit-testable in isolation per AC#4), and simplicity (a dict keyed by the literal names the specs already carry). Alternative (a) loses on the typo-detection goal (no central key set to validate against); (b) adds gratuitous indirection for forced-shape pure predicates. Alternatives discarded — no coaching finding.

- POSITIVE RATIONALE present (G6(4)). The "What" states why the chosen approach fits: "Build the executable substrate the specs name but that does not exist yet" — a positive fit rationale, not a rejected-alternatives section. Satisfied.

FINDING (G6 — approach-fitness / single-source-of-truth: the emits_valid_* scorers should DELEGATE to the shipped contract registry rather than re-implement shape-validation):
- [ ] Implement the `emits_valid_findings` / `emits_valid_review_result` / `emits_valid_verification` scorers as thin wrappers over the ALREADY-SHIPPED contract registry (`rebar.llm.contracts.response_model_for(name)` -> `model.model_validate(structured_output)`), instead of writing bespoke field-by-field shape-validation in the new scorer module — so "the output validates against the X contract" reuses ONE source of truth and cannot drift from the JSON-Schema-pin tests that keep each Pydantic model in lock-step. The right approach binds each scorer to a registered contract NAME (emits_valid_findings -> plan_review_findings; emits_valid_review_result -> review_result; emits_valid_verification -> plan_review_verification / completion_verdict). A second hand-rolled validator is the structurally-inferior choice here: it forks the schema definition and silently diverges the day a contract field changes.
  - criteria: [G6]
  - location: plan "What" §1 (scorer registry naming emits_valid_findings/emits_valid_review_result/emits_valid_verdict); plan AC#1; src/rebar/llm/contracts.py:24-39,88-89; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19-20
  - evidence: A name->builder registry mapping a serializable NAME to the Pydantic model the output must validate against ALREADY EXISTS and is the documented reuse seam — `register_contract(name, builder)` + `response_model_for(output_schema)` (contracts.py:24-39); the exact contracts these scorers check are already registered (plan_review_findings / plan_review_verification; review_result / completion_verdict, contracts.py:88-89). The finder spec already DESCRIBES the scorer as "The output validates against the plan_review_findings contract" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19-20). The chosen-vs-better-approach axis: re-implementing shape-validation in eval_scorers.py is a clearly-inferior structural choice (two sources of truth, drift risk vs the schema-pin tests at contracts.py:9-10) when a thin delegating wrapper over response_model_for is simpler AND robust. (Note: the plan's name `emits_valid_verdict` does not match the shipped `emits_valid_verification` — a separate G1G2 edit-target matter, out of G6 scope.)
  - suggested_fix: In the registry, `def emits_valid_findings(case, out): return _validates(out, "plan_review_findings")` where `_validates(out, name) = contracts.response_model_for(name).model_validate(out) is not None` (try/except -> False) — one wrapper per registered contract name, no bespoke per-field checks.

No further G6 findings: the registry abstraction, the (case, structured_output) signature, the offline/deterministic boundary, the alternative-selection, and the positive rationale are all sound.
